option.name: updatedContent
option.value:
import re
def strip_markdown(text): text = re.sub(r'\*{1,2}(.*?)\*{1,2}', r'\1', text) text = re.sub(r'_{1,2}(.*?)_{1,2}', r'\1', text) text = re.sub(r'`(.*?)`', r'\1', text) return text
tests = [ ('**/end ticket** VM-000005', True), ('/end ticket VM-000005', True), ('**Some other text**', False), ('Hello world', False), ('__/end ticket__ VM-000005', True), ('/End Ticket VM-000005', True), ]
for raw, expected in tests: clean = strip_markdown(raw).strip().lower() result = clean.startswith('/end ticket') status = 'OK' if result == expected else 'FAIL' print(f'{status} | raw={raw!r:40s} | clean={clean!r}')
